$comment: Runnable eval definitions for /pr (spec docs/spec-portable-ai-procedures.md §4.2). Converted from the original repo-root evals/evals.json per the approved conversion manifest (docs/spec-skill-evals-manifest.md — Phase 1 of docs/spec-skill-evals-baseline.md). EXECUTION NOTICE: skill-eval prompts are never executed against the real repo or real GitHub — any skill, any origin. `kind: execution` entries require a harness-built sandbox (`fixture` names the starting-state profile; `human_script` gives the scripted human replies, valid only inside a marker-bearing sandbox) — see docs/strategy-skill-evals.md. `kind: routing` entries test whether/how the skill fires in a live session; a subagent handed this file cannot execute them — they stay documented manual runbooks (docs/strategy-skill-evals.md) until the Phase-8 session runner. As of Phase 1 the sandbox harness does not exist yet (it ships in Phase 2), so no eval below is executable at all right now — CLAUDE.md's guardrail carries this staged caveat too.

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch that has an open PR; 2 new commits ahead of the last push; working tree clean; gh authenticated; `origin/main` has not advanced (rebase is a no-op).
evals.human_script: None — no human approval checkpoint is reached on this path: the run posts a PR-conversation comment automatically and skips the body update because there is no material scope change.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs `gh auth status` (pass), confirms tree clean, `git fetch origin main`, `merge-base --is-ancestor` succeeds (no rebase needed), skips the post-rebase `npm test` re-run (rebase was a no-op), `git push -u origin <branch>`, posts a PR-conversation comment summarizing each new commit (one bullet per commit), does NOT update the PR body (no material scope change), prints the PR URL, stops. Does NOT poll CI checks.
expectations: `gh auth status` runs and succeeds before any git/gh mutation
`git fetch origin main` runs, and `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main HEAD` succeeds so no rebase occurs
`npm test` does not re-run (the rebase was a no-op)
`git push -u origin <branch>` appears in the command log
The gh call log shows exactly one PR-conversation comment posted, with one bullet per new commit
The gh call log shows no PR body-update call
The gh call log contains no CI-check polling call (e.g. no `gh pr checks`)
The final output prints the PR URL

evals.preconditions: Current checkout is on branch `feature-x`. PR 145 is on branch `feature-y`. Tree clean; gh authenticated.
evals.human_script: None — the skill refuses before any interactive checkpoint is reached; no scripted reply is required.
evals.expected_output: Skill resolves the argument to PR 145 via `gh pr view 145`, detects the branch mismatch, refuses to switch (per `/pr` policy), names both branches in the refusal, and suggests `/ship 145` for switching. No fetch, no push, no PR-side mutation.
expectations: `gh pr view 145` appears in the gh call log
The response names both branches (`feature-x` and `feature-y`) in the refusal
The response suggests `/ship 145` as the switching path
The command/gh call log contains no `git fetch`, no `git push`, and no PR-mutating call (no `gh pr create`, no PR comment, no body update)

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with uncommitted changes related to a dashboard wiring task; no existing PR; gh authenticated.
evals.human_script: At /commit's step-14 approval checkpoint (reached via delegation), the human replies: y. At /pr's PR-draft approval checkpoint (title + body + reviewers), the human replies: y.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects dirty tree at the working-tree pre-flight, delegates to `/commit "wire up dashboard"` with the same argument. As it writes the delegation marker and invokes `/commit`, `/pr` prints `Using /commit — delegated from /pr` as the handoff line — and the delegated `/commit` does NOT separately self-announce (the marker suppresses its self-announcement), so the hop is announced exactly once, by the parent, even though `/pr` here was typed as a slash command. `/commit` runs its full flow (stage, test, approval, commit, push). On return, `/pr` continues: fetch, no-op rebase, no-op re-test, push (already pushed by `/commit`; idempotent), then drafts a CC-format PR title (e.g., `feat: wire up dashboard`) and structured body for human approval, runs `gh pr create` on approval, prints the URL, stops.
expectations: `/pr` detects the dirty tree at the working-tree pre-flight and delegates to `/commit "wire up dashboard"` with the same argument
The transcript shows the delegation marker written and `/pr` printing `Using /commit — delegated from /pr` as the handoff line before the delegated call
The delegated `/commit` does not print its own `Using /commit` announcement (self-announcement suppressed by the marker)
`/commit` runs its full flow: stages the dashboard-related paths, runs `npm test`, presents the approval block, commits, and pushes — exactly one new commit exists in the sandbox git log
After `/commit` returns, `/pr` continues with fetch and a no-op rebase/re-test, and the subsequent push is idempotent (no error from re-pushing an already-current branch)
`/pr` drafts a Conventional-Commit-style PR title (e.g. `feat: wire up dashboard`) and a structured body, presents it for approval, and on approval runs exactly one `gh pr create` call
The final output prints the PR URL

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with no open PR; tree clean; the branch contains a single commit that removes a public API field (a breaking change); gh authenticated; `origin/main` has not advanced (rebase no-op).
evals.human_script: At the PR-draft approval checkpoint, the human replies: y
evals.expected_output: Skill runs `gh auth status`, confirms tree clean, fetch, no-op rebase, no-op re-test, push, then drafts a CC-format title with the breaking-change flag — e.g., `feat!: remove deprecated profile.legacyId field` (≤70 chars). Body includes the `Summary:` / `Why:` / `Behavior:` / `Test Plan:` sections and a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer explaining the compatibility impact. Surfaces the breaking-change classification in the approval prompt so the human can confirm before `gh pr create`.
expectations: `gh auth status` runs and succeeds; tree is confirmed clean
Fetch and a no-op rebase occur (origin/main not advanced); `npm test` does not re-run
`git push -u origin <branch>` appears in the command log
The drafted PR title uses `!` before the colon and is ≤70 chars (e.g. `feat!: remove deprecated profile.legacyId field`)
The drafted body includes `Summary:`, `Why:`, `Behavior:`, `Test Plan:` sections and a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer explaining the compatibility impact
The approval prompt surfaces the breaking-change classification before `gh pr create` runs
On approval, exactly one `gh pr create` call appears in the gh call log

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with a single commit and no open PR; tree clean; `origin/main` not advanced (rebase no-op); gh authenticated; `.claude/skills/pr/.team-cache.json` does not exist (cold cache); the repo has 5 human collaborators after bot-filtering.
evals.human_script: At the reviewer picker prompt, the human replies: 1 3. At the bundled approval block (title + body + reviewers), the human replies: y.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs Steps 1-8 normally. At Step 9, detects no cache and fires the three cold-cache fetches in parallel: `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/collaborators`, `gh api user`, and parallel `gh api users/<login>` for display names. Applies the bot filter to the collaborator list before writing `.claude/skills/pr/.team-cache.json` with `collaborators`, `displayNames`, `self`, `refreshedAt`. Renders the vertical picker sorted alphabetically by display name (case-insensitive), with `self` excluded and entries numbered in sort order. The rendered picker MUST NOT contain `[xN]` count markers, 'Frequent reviewers' or 'Other collaborators' tier headers, or any sort key derived from past review/author activity. Human types `1 3`; Skill resolves to the two corresponding logins, echoes `→ Requesting review from <login1>, <login3>. Proceeding.`, presents the bundled approval block (title + body + resolved reviewers), and on approval runs `gh pr create --reviewer <login1>,<login3>` (no `@`, no spaces inside the value).
expectations: No `.claude/skills/pr/.team-cache.json` exists at sandbox start (cold-cache liveness: the file's absence is verified before the run, not assumed)
The gh call log shows the three cold-cache fetches fired for the fixture's 5 human collaborators (`gh api repos/.../collaborators`, `gh api user`, per-login `gh api users/<login>`)
The bot filter is applied before the cache is written — no bot/app login appears in the rendered picker
`.claude/skills/pr/.team-cache.json` is written with `collaborators`, `displayNames`, `self`, `refreshedAt`

The rendered picker is sorted alphabetically by display name (case-insensitive), excludes `self`, is numbered in sort order, and contains no `[xN]` count markers or 'Frequent reviewers'/'Other collaborators' tier headers
Given the human's reply `1 3`, the echoed resolution line names the two corresponding logins: `→ Requesting review from <login1>, <login3>. Proceeding.`
The bundled approval block presents title + body + resolved reviewers before `gh pr create` runs
On approval, `gh pr create --reviewer <login1>,<login3>` appears in the gh call log with no `@` prefix and no spaces inside the value

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with a single commit and no open PR; tree clean; `origin/main` not advanced (rebase no-op); gh authenticated; `.claude/skills/pr/.team-cache.json` exists, `refreshedAt` is 3 days old (well within 15-day TTL), `collaborators` includes both `james-baker` (displayName: `James Baker`) and `benjifriedman` (displayName: `Benji Friedman`), `self` is the running human's login.
evals.human_script: At the reviewer picker prompt, the human replies: james and benji. At the bundled approval block, the human replies: y.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads the cache without firing any `gh api` calls. Renders the vertical picker sorted alphabetically by display name with `self` excluded. The rendered picker MUST NOT contain `[xN]` count markers, 'Frequent reviewers' or 'Other collaborators' tier headers, or any sort key derived from past review/author activity. Human types `james and benji`; Skill matches against the cached `displayNames` dictionary, resolves unambiguously to `[james-baker, benjifriedman]`, asks no clarifying question, echoes `→ Requesting review from james-baker, benjifriedman. Proceeding.`, and on approval runs `gh pr create --reviewer james-baker,benjifriedman`. No cache write occurs during this run.
expectations: `.claude/skills/pr/.team-cache.json` exists at sandbox start with `refreshedAt` 3 days old (within the 15-day TTL)
Liveness check before trusting the negative assertion below: the gh call log is non-empty overall (e.g. contains `gh auth status`), proving the stub was exercised and PATH is wired correctly
The gh call log shows zero `gh api` calls (collaborator/user/display-name lookups) during this run
The rendered picker is sorted alphabetically by display name, excludes `self`, is numbered in sort order, with no `[xN]` markers or tier headers
Given the human's reply `james and benji`, the skill resolves unambiguously to `[james-baker, benjifriedman]` and asks no clarifying question
The echoed resolution line reads `→ Requesting review from james-baker, benjifriedman. Proceeding.`
On approval, `gh pr create --reviewer james-baker,benjifriedman` appears in the gh call log
No cache-write occurs during this run — the cache file's `refreshedAt` is unchanged after the run

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with a single commit and no open PR; tree clean; `origin/main` not advanced (rebase no-op); gh authenticated; `.claude/skills/pr/.team-cache.json` exists with `refreshedAt` 5 days old (within TTL) and contains a login `formerteam` that has since been removed from the repo's collaborator list on GitHub.
evals.human_script: At the first reviewer picker prompt, the human picks the number corresponding to `formerteam`. After the refreshed picker re-renders, the human picks a valid login from the refreshed list. At the bundled approval block, the human replies: y.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads the cache, renders the picker including `formerteam`. Human picks the number corresponding to `formerteam`. Skill echoes `→ Requesting review from formerteam. Proceeding.` and calls `gh pr create --reviewer formerteam`. gh returns a reviewer-invalid error. Skill catches the error, surfaces the exact `gh` error text to the human, refreshes the team cache via the same three cold-cache fetches (after which `formerteam` no longer appears in `collaborators`), re-renders the picker with the refreshed list, and re-asks for a reviewer pick. Does NOT retry `gh pr create` with the same input. No PR is created until the re-asked picker resolves to a valid set; on the re-asked pick, `gh pr create --reviewer <valid-login>` runs and the PR opens.
expectations: The cache exists with `refreshedAt` 5 days old and includes `formerteam`; the rendered picker includes `formerteam` as an option
Given the human's first pick, the echoed resolution line reads `→ Requesting review from formerteam. Proceeding.` and `gh pr create --reviewer formerteam` appears in the gh call log
The stub returns a reviewer-invalid error for that specific call (per-call sequenced response — named Phase-2 stub input) and the skill surfaces the exact error text to the human
The skill refreshes the team cache via the same three cold-cache fetches (evidenced in the gh call log), after which `formerteam` no longer appears in the re-rendered picker
The skill re-asks for a reviewer pick and does NOT retry `gh pr create --reviewer formerteam` again
On the re-asked valid pick, exactly one further `gh pr create --reviewer <valid-login>` call appears in the gh call log, and the PR opens

evals.prompt: open a PR for this branch
evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with commits to push and no open PR; tree may be clean or dirty; gh authenticated; no `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local` opt-out file; not a delegated call (no `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` marker); invoked by natural language (Skill-tool invocation, not a typed `/pr`).
evals.expected_output: The /pr Skill is invoked (NL intent routed through the Skill). Its first visible line is the announcement `Using /pr`. Its first ACTION (after that line) is the Step 0 intent gate via AskUserQuestion ("Open a PR with: <detected context>?") — no `gh auth`, no `git fetch`/`push`, no `gh pr create` before it. Choosing Stop ends cleanly with zero side effects (no push, no PR created). Choosing Proceed continues into step 1 onward.
expectations: The first visible line of the response is the announcement `Using /pr`
The Step 0 intent gate fires via AskUserQuestion before any `gh auth`, `git fetch`/`push`, or `gh pr create`
Choosing Stop leaves zero side effects: no push, no PR created
Choosing Proceed continues into step 1 onward
evals.notes: Routing eval — not sandbox-executable by a subagent (routing is the thing under test). Stays a documented manual runbook at baseline; automated by the Phase-8 session runner.

evals.prompt: (assistant turn) "Want me to open a PR for this?" then (human turn) "yes"
evals.preconditions: Mid-conversation: the assistant has just pushed commits and offered to open a PR; the human replies only with a bare affirmation ("yes" / "go ahead") and does NOT repeat "PR". On a feature branch with commits and no open PR; gh authenticated; no `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local`; no `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` marker.
evals.expected_output: The bare affirmation is treated as the PR trigger: the model invokes the /pr Skill via the Skill tool and does NOT hand-roll the PR with an ad-hoc `git push` + `gh pr create`. The first visible line is the announcement `Using /pr`, followed by the Step 0 intent gate. Guards against the model reading a bare "yes" to its own offer as approval of an ad-hoc plan and opening the PR directly, with no visible sign the Skill ran.
expectations: The bare affirmation routes through the Skill tool — no ad-hoc `git push` + `gh pr create` sequence appears anywhere in the transcript
The first visible line is the announcement `Using /pr`
The Step 0 intent gate fires immediately after the announcement
evals.notes: Routing eval — two-turn; the Phase-8 multi-turn driver case (R4). Not sandbox-executable by a subagent at baseline.
